questions.question: Which is the longest river in the world?
questions.explanation: The Nile River, flowing through northeastern Africa, is traditionally considered the longest river in the world at about 6,650 kilometers.

questions.question: Which is the largest continent by area?
questions.explanation: Asia is the largest continent by both area (about 44.58 million sq km) and population (over 4.6 billion people).

questions.question: What is the capital of Australia?
questions.explanation: Canberra is the capital city of Australia, located in the Australian Capital Territory (ACT). It was chosen as a compromise between Sydney and Melbourne.

questions.question: Which mountain is the tallest above sea level?
questions.explanation: Mount Everest, located in the Himalayas on the border of Nepal and Tibet, is Earth's highest mountain above sea level at 8,848.86 meters.

questions.question: Which desert is the largest hot desert in the world?
questions.explanation: The Sahara Desert in northern Africa is the largest hot desert in the world, covering about 9.2 million square kilometers.

questions.question: What is the smallest country in the world by area?
questions.explanation: Vatican City is the smallest independent state in the world, with an area of about 0.49 square kilometers.

questions.question: Which ocean is the largest?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest of Earth's oceans, covering about 165 million square kilometers.

questions.question: Which country has the most time zones?
questions.explanation: France has 12 time zones when including its overseas territories, more than any other country.

questions.question: What is the capital city of Canada?
questions.explanation: Ottawa is the capital city of Canada, located in the province of Ontario.

questions.question: Which river flows through Egypt?
questions.explanation: The Nile River flows through Egypt and is the lifeblood of the country, supporting agriculture and civilization along its banks.

questions.question: What is the value of pi to two decimal places?
questions.explanation: Pi is approximately 3.14159..., commonly rounded to 3.14 for everyday use.

questions.question: What is 7 multiplied by 8?
questions.explanation: 7 multiplied by 8 equals 56.

questions.question: What is the square root of 144?
questions.explanation: The square root of 144 is 12, because 12 multiplied by 12 equals 144.

questions.question: What is the next prime number after 7?
questions.explanation: The next prime number after 7 is 11. A prime number has only two distinct positive divisors: 1 and itself.

questions.question: What is the Pythagorean theorem?
questions.explanation: The Pythagorean theorem states that in a right-angled triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of squares of the other two sides: a^2 + b^2 = c^2.

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of a circle?
questions.explanation: The area of a circle is calculated by the formula A = pi*r^2, where r is the radius.

questions.question: How many degrees are in a triangle's interior angles?
questions.explanation: The sum of the interior angles of any triangle is always 180 degrees in Euclidean geometry.

questions.question: What is the derivative of x^2 with respect to x?
questions.explanation: Using the power rule for differentiation, the derivative of x^2 with respect to x is 2x.

questions.question: What is 15% of 200?
questions.explanation: 15% of 200 is calculated as 0.15 * 200 = 30.

questions.question: What is the value of e (Euler's number) to two decimal places?
questions.explanation: Euler's number, e, is approximately 2.71828..., commonly rounded to 2.71 or 2.72.

questions.question: Who wrote the play 'Romeo and Juliet'?
questions.explanation: 'Romeo and Juliet' was written by William Shakespeare around 1594-1596 and is one of his most famous tragedies.

questions.question: Who wrote '1984' and 'Animal Farm'?
questions.explanation: George Orwell (pen name of Eric Arthur Blair) wrote '1984' (1949) and 'Animal Farm' (1945), both political allegories.

questions.question: Which Russian author wrote 'War and Peace'?
questions.explanation: 'War and Peace' was written by Leo Tolstoy and published in 1869. It is considered one of the greatest novels ever written.

questions.question: Who wrote 'Pride and Prejudice'?
questions.explanation: 'Pride and Prejudice' was written by Jane Austen and first published in 1813.

questions.question: Who is the author of 'The Great Gatsby'?
questions.explanation: 'The Great Gatsby' was written by F. Scott Fitzgerald and published in 1925, set in the Jazz Age on Long Island.

questions.question: Who wrote the 'Harry Potter' series?
questions.explanation: J.K. Rowling wrote the seven-book 'Harry Potter' series, with the first book published in 1997.

questions.question: Which novel begins with 'Call me Ishmael'?
options: The Old Man and the Sea
questions.explanation: 'Call me Ishmael' is the famous opening line of Herman Melville's novel 'Moby-Dick', published in 1851.

questions.question: Who wrote 'To Kill a Mockingbird'?
questions.explanation: 'To Kill a Mockingbird' was written by Harper Lee and published in 1960. It won the Pulitzer Prize for Fiction in 1961.

questions.question: Who wrote 'The Lord of the Rings'?
questions.explanation: 'The Lord of the Rings' was written by J.R.R. Tolkien and published in three volumes from 1954 to 1955.

questions.question: Who is the author of 'Crime and Punishment'?
questions.explanation: 'Crime and Punishment' was written by Fyodor Dostoevsky and first published in 1866. It's considered one of the greatest psychological novels.

questions.question: How many players are on a standard soccer (football) team?
questions.explanation: A standard soccer/football team has 11 players on the field, including the goalkeeper.

questions.question: In which sport would you perform a 'slam dunk'?
questions.explanation: A slam dunk is performed in basketball when a player jumps and forcefully puts the ball through the basket.

questions.question: How often are the Summer Olympic Games held?
questions.explanation: The Summer Olympic Games are held every 4 years, though the 2020 Tokyo games were delayed to 2021 due to COVID-19.

questions.question: Which country invented the sport of cricket?
questions.explanation: Cricket originated in England, with the first recorded match dating back to the 16th century.

questions.question: How many points is a touchdown worth in American football?
questions.explanation: A touchdown in American football is worth 6 points. An extra point or two-point conversion can be added afterward.

questions.question: Which tennis tournament is played on grass courts?
questions.explanation: Wimbledon, the oldest tennis tournament in the world, is the only Grand Slam played on grass courts.

questions.question: How many holes are played in a standard round of golf?
questions.explanation: A standard round of golf consists of 18 holes.

questions.question: Which boxer was famously known as 'The Greatest'?
questions.explanation: Muhammad Ali, three-time world heavyweight champion, was famously known as 'The Greatest' and is considered one of the most significant athletes of the 20th century.

questions.question: In Formula 1, what does 'pole position' mean?
options: First place on the starting grid
questions.explanation: Pole position is the first place position on the starting grid in motor racing, awarded to the fastest driver in qualifying.

questions.question: Which country has won the most FIFA World Cups?
questions.explanation: Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup a record 5 times: 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, and 2002.
